question: In which type of tree traversal is the root node visited after all of its subtrees have been visited?

answers.feedback: Incorrect. In preorder traversal, the root node is visited first, before the subtrees.
Incorrect. In inorder traversal, we visit the left subtree, then the root, then the right subtree.
Correct! Postorder visits the left subtree, then the right subtree, and finally the root node.
Incorrect. Level-order visits nodes layer by layer from top to bottom.
